lesson.menu_title: More, Less, the Best — Comparing HR Data and Business Performance
lesson.menu_desc: Comparing HR data and performance with comparatives and superlatives -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 7 -- More, Less, the Best -- Comparatives and Superlatives
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 7 -- More, Less, the Best

listenings.text: Hi Murielle, it's Liam. Let me compare our two regions before the review, so the numbers are clear. First, our turnover in Australia is lower than in the other regions. It is nine percent, which is the lowest rate in the company. Second, our hiring is faster than last year. The time to fill a role is shorter now, about three weeks. However, I have to be honest: our cost per hire is higher than the European benchmark. It is more expensive because the market is smaller and more competitive. The good news is that retention is better than before, and the trend is positive. In one line: we are more efficient than last year, but Europe is still the most cost-effective region. Let's talk about how to close that gap. Talk soon.

Good morning, Murielle. This is Diana from London, with a quick ranking before the board meeting. I looked at the metrics from all five regions. On engagement, Australia has the highest score, which is excellent. On cost, Europe is the most cost-effective, so we should learn from them. The biggest gap is in onboarding time: some regions are much faster than others. Our best region takes two weeks, but our slowest region takes six. That difference is too large. My recommendation is simple: the fastest region is going to share its process, and every other region is going to follow the same benchmark. If we do this, next year is going to be our strongest year for talent. I am sending you the full ranking now. Thank you.
